title: Rename AttackStep to WebAttackStep (name collision with JWTAttackStep)
description: web-attacks.ts:18 exports `AttackStep`. jwt-attacks.ts:19 exports `JWTAttackStep`. SecurityModule.tsx:69 already aliases: `import type { AttackStep as WebAttackStep }`. Rename at source to avoid confusion. This is a compile-safe refactor.
acceptanceCriteria: web-attacks.ts exports `WebAttackStep` (not `AttackStep`)
All imports updated (SecurityModule.tsx, index.ts, tests)
No functional change — pure rename

title: Narrow actor fields from string to union literals in OAuthStep, DHStep, EncryptionStep
description: OAuthStep.actor is `string` but only takes 4 values ('User Agent','Client','Auth Server','Resource Server'). DHStep.actor is `string` but only takes 4 values. EncryptionStep.actor is `string` but only takes 4 values. AttackStep already uses a proper union — follow its pattern. Narrow to: `actor: 'User Agent' | 'Client' | 'Auth Server' | 'Resource Server'` etc. This makes oauthActorToColumn() and similar functions type-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: OAuthStep.actor uses 4-member union literal
DHStep.actor uses 4-member union literal ('Public'|'Alice'|'Bob'|'Both')
EncryptionStep.actor uses 4-member union literal ('Alice'|'Bob'|'Network'|'Both')
oauthActorToColumn() and similar functions type-check without string comparison

title: Narrow OAuthStep.httpMethod from string to union literal
description: OAuthStep.httpMethod is `string?` but only takes values 'GET','POST','302','200','400'. Narrow to `httpMethod?: 'GET' | 'POST' | '302' | '200' | '400'`. This prevents typos and enables exhaustive pattern matching in the visualizer.
acceptanceCriteria: httpMethod uses 5-member union literal
All OAuthStep instances in oauth-flows.ts and device-auth.ts type-check
No string comparison needed in rendering code

title: Refactor SecurityPropertiesContextValue from 60-field flat interface to discriminated union
description: SecurityModule.tsx:2843-2911 defines SecurityPropertiesContextValue with 60+ fields for all 11 topics. Adding a new topic requires adding 5-10 fields to this interface, the provider value, and the consumer destructure. Refactor to discriminated union: `type SecurityPropertiesCtx = { active: 'oauth'; steps: OAuthStep[]; ... } | { active: 'jwt'; header: string; ... } | ...`. Each topic's properties component only sees its own fields — type-safe and extensible.
acceptanceCriteria: SecurityPropertiesContextValue is a discriminated union on `active`
Each topic's SecurityProperties case only destructures its own fields
Adding a new topic only requires adding a new union member
TypeScript catches if a topic's properties panel accesses wrong fields

title: Add exhaustive switch check (assertNever) to canvas topic switch
description: SecurityModule.tsx canvas switch at ~line 4880 has a `default:` case showing a placeholder. This means adding a new SecurityTopic member does NOT cause a compile error — the default silently catches it. Replace default with assertNever(active) exhaustive check. TypeScript will then flag any new topic that isn't handled in the switch.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas switch statement has no default case
assertNever(active) at the end causes compile error for unhandled topics
Adding 'ssrf' to SecurityTopic union causes compile error until canvas case is added
Same pattern applied to SecurityProperties and SecurityBottomPanel switches

title: Add parseHex validation with Result type instead of silent zero-fill
description: aes-engine.ts parseHex returns number[] but silently converts invalid hex to 0. Add a Result type: `function parseHex(hex: string): { ok: true; bytes: number[] } | { ok: false; error: string; position: number }`. Callers must check the result. This is a type-level fix for the runtime bug found in mega audit (SEC-023/SEC-146).
acceptanceCriteria: parseHex returns a discriminated Result type
Invalid hex characters produce an error with position info
AES properties panel shows the error when parseHex fails
Valid hex still produces correct number[]

title: Standardize step index naming across all topics
description: Step indices use inconsistent names: `stepIndex` (OAuth/DH/HTTPS), `aesStateIndex` (AES), `attackStepIndex` (JWT Attacks), `encryptionStepIndex` (Encryption). Standardize to a consistent pattern. Options: (a) all use `stepIndex` with topic-qualified context, (b) all use `{topic}StepIndex` consistently. The current mix makes the useSecurityModule hook harder to read and maintain.
acceptanceCriteria: All step index variables follow a consistent naming pattern
Pattern documented for future topics
No functional change — pure rename

title: Extract BaseSecurityStep interface shared across all step types
description: All 12 step types have `description: string`. Many share `actor`, `tick`, or other fields. Extract a base: `interface BaseSecurityStep { description: string; }` and have each step type extend it. This enables generic step rendering and shared step utilities without losing per-type specificity.
acceptanceCriteria: BaseSecurityStep interface defined with shared fields (at minimum: description)
At least 6 step types extend BaseSecurityStep
Generic step rendering utility works with BaseSecurityStep
Per-type fields preserved via intersection or extension
